![HTML5](https://img.shields.io/badge/HTML5-E34F26?style=flat&logo=html5&logoColor=white) ![CSS3](https://img.shields.io/badge/CSS3-1572B6?style=flat&logo=css3&logoColor=white) ![Google Fonts](https://img.shields.io/badge/Google%20Fonts-4285F4?style=flat&logo=googlefonts&logoColor=white) ![MIT License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green?style=flat) ![Vibe Coded badge](https://img.shields.io/static/v1?message=Vibe%20Coded&labelColor=5c5c5c&color=7c3aed&logoColor=white&label=%20&style=for-the-badge&logo=data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

# miamaiers-personal-landing-page

## Description

A responsive personal landing page for Mia Maiers, a Business Analytics and Information Systems junior at the University of Iowa. The site serves as a digital portfolio that bridges technical capability with business communication, showcasing academic achievements, analytical skills, and data-driven projects. It is built for leadership at Decker Concrete and Mia's professional network in Iowa.

## Features

- Professional hero section with circular headshot and tagline
- About section highlighting academic standing, honors, and career path
- Skills section with color-coded pill badges for tools and analytical techniques
- Three featured project cards with descriptions and technology tags
- Contact section with linked icon badges for LinkedIn, GitHub, and email
- Fully responsive layout from 320px mobile to desktop widths
- Sticky navigation bar with smooth-scrolling anchor links
- WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessible design with semantic HTML5

## Tech Stack

| Technology | Purpose |
|---|---|
| HTML5 | Page structure using semantic elements (`<header>`, `<main>`, `<section>`, `<article>`, `<footer>`, `<nav>`) |
| CSS3 | All visual styling via external stylesheet with CSS custom properties |
| Google Fonts (DM Sans) | Professional, readable typeface loaded via CDN |
| Python 3 (http.server) | Local development server |
| GitHub | Version control and repository hosting |
| Replit | Cloud development environment and static deployment |

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- A modern web browser ([Chrome](https://www.google.com/chrome/), [Firefox](https://www.mozilla.org/firefox/), or [Safari](https://www.apple.com/safari/))
- [Git](https://git-scm.com/downloads) installed on your machine
- [Python 3](https://www.python.org/downloads/) installed (for the local dev server) **or** any static file server

### Installation

1. Clone the repository:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/miamaiers/miamaiers-personal-landing-page.git
```

2. Navigate into the project folder:

```bash
cd miamaiers-personal-landing-page
```

3. Start a local development server:

```bash
python3 -m http.server 5000
```

4. Open your browser and visit:

```
http://localhost:5000
```

## Usage

Open the page in any browser. Use the sticky navigation bar at the top to jump to any section: About, Skills, Projects, or Contact. The site is read-only with no configuration needed. All external links (LinkedIn, GitHub, email) open in a new tab.

## Project Structure

```
miamaiers-personal-landing-page/
├── index.html              # Main HTML page with all content sections
├── css/
│   └── stylesheet.css      # All visual styles and responsive breakpoints
├── js/
│   └── scripts.js          # Reserved for future JavaScript (currently empty)
├── images/
│   └── headshot.jpeg       # Professional headshot displayed in the hero section
├── PRD.md                  # Product requirements document
├── STANDARDS.md            # Technical and design standards for the project
└── README.md               # This file
```

## Changelog

### v1.0.0 — 2026-03-12

- Initial release of the personal landing page
- Hero section with professional headshot, name, and tagline
- About section with academic and career details
- Skills section with tool and technique pill badges
- Three project cards: Iowa City Police Blotter Dashboard, Heart Attack Susceptibility Analysis, Aflac Churn Project
- Contact section with LinkedIn, GitHub, and email icon badges
- Responsive layout tested at 320px, 480px, 768px, and 1024px
- WCAG 2.2 Level AA compliance (color contrast, heading hierarchy, alt text, keyboard navigation)

## Known Issues / To-Do

- [ ] Add a favicon for browser tab branding
- [ ] Add Open Graph meta tags for better link previews when shared on social media
- [ ] Add smooth scroll offset to account for the sticky nav bar height when clicking anchor links
- [ ] Create a `LICENSE` file in the repository root

## Roadmap

- Add a downloadable resume button (PDF generated from site content, not a static upload)
- Integrate live project demos or embedded dashboards for each project card
- Add a dark mode toggle with saved preference via `localStorage`
- Include a blog or case study section to narrate the "why" behind each project
- Add page analytics to track unique visitor count toward the 10-visitor goal

## Contributing

Contributions are welcome. If you have suggestions for improvements or find a bug, follow these steps:

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name`)
3. Commit your changes (`git commit -m "Add your feature description"`)
4. Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/your-feature-name`)
5. Open a Pull Request describing what you changed and why
